message:
perf(output): zero-allocation JSON length measurement for TOON stats

Replace serde_json::to_string_pretty(&value).len() with a CountingWriter
that implements std::io::Write by accumulating byte counts without
allocating a String.  The pretty-printed JSON size is only used for
the optional TOON stats line on stderr, so the full serialized string
was immediately discarded — pure waste on large outputs.

CountingWriter is a private struct with a const fn len() accessor.
pretty_json_len() drives serde_json::Serializer::pretty through it
and returns the byte count.

Add a unit test (pretty_json_len_matches_pretty_serializer_output)
that verifies the CountingWriter result matches the old to_string_pretty
approach for a representative nested JSON value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
